 5â†’/**
 6â†’ * Builds function to locate position in text - line and column based on offset from the text start.
 7â†’ */
 8â†’internal fun buildPositionInTextLocator(text: String): (offset: Int) -> LineAndColumn {
 9â†’    val textLength = text.length
10â†’    val arr = ArrayList<Int>()
11â†’
12â†’    var endOfLineIndex = -1
13â†’    do {
14â†’        arr.add(endOfLineIndex + 1)
15â†’        endOfLineIndex = text.indexOf('\n', endOfLineIndex + 1)
16â†’    } while (endOfLineIndex != -1)
17â†’
18â†’    arr.add(textLength + if (arr.last() == textLength) 1 else 0)
19â†’
20â†’    val segmentTree = SegmentTree(arr.toTypedArray())
21â†’
22â†’    return { offset ->
23â†’        val line = segmentTree.indexOf(offset)
24â†’        if (line != -1) {
25â†’            val col = offset - segmentTree.get(line).left
26â†’            line + 1 to col + 1
27â†’        } else {
28â†’            1 to 1
29â†’        }
30â†’    }
31â†’}

33â†’private class SegmentTree(
34â†’    sortedArray: Array<Int>,
35â†’) {
36â†’    init {
37â†’        require(sortedArray.size > 1) { "At least two data points are required" }
38â†’        sortedArray.reduce { current, next ->
39â†’            require(current <= next) { "Data points are not sorted (ASC)" }
40â†’            next
41â†’        }
42â†’    }
43â†’
44â†’    private val segments: List<Segment> =
45â†’        sortedArray
46â†’            .dropLast(1)
47â†’            .mapIndexed { index: Int, element: Int ->
48â†’                Segment(element, sortedArray[index + 1] - 1)
49â†’            }
50â†’
51â†’    fun get(i: Int): Segment = segments[i]
52â†’
53â†’    fun indexOf(v: Int): Int = binarySearch(v, 0, segments.size - 1)
54â†’
55â†’    private fun binarySearch(
56â†’        v: Int,
57â†’        l: Int,
58â†’        r: Int,
59â†’    ): Int =
60â†’        when {
61â†’            l > r -> -1
62â†’            else -> {
63â†’                val i = l + (r - l) / 2
64â†’                val s = segments[i]
65â†’                if (v < s.left) {
66â†’                    binarySearch(v, l, i - 1)
67â†’                } else {
68â†’                    if (s.right < v) binarySearch(v, i + 1, r) else i
69â†’                }
70â†’            }
71â†’        }
72â†’}

75â†’    val left: Int,
76â†’    val right: Int,
77â†’)

1â†’# Changelog
  2â†’All notable changes to this project will be documented in this file.
  3â†’This project adheres to [Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org/).
  4â†’
  5â†’## Unreleased
  6â†’
  7â†’### Custom Rule Providers need to prepare for Kotlin 1.9
  8â†’
  9â†’In Kotlin 1.9 the extension points of the embedded kotlin compiler will change. Ktlint only uses the `org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler` extension point. This extension is not yet supported in 1.9, neither is it documented ([#KT-58704](https://youtrack.jetbrains.com/issue/KT-58704/Support-and-document-extension-point-org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler)). Without this extension point it might happen that your custom rules will throw exceptions during runtime. See [#1981](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1981).
 10â†’
 11â†’In Ktlint, 7 out of 77 rules needed small and sometimes bigger changes to become independent of the extension point `org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler`. The impact on your custom rules may vary dependent on the way the autocorrect has been implemented. When manipulating `ASTNode`s there seems to be no impact. When, manipulating `PsiElement`s, some functions consistently result in a runtime exception.
 12â†’
 13â†’Based on the refactoring of the rules as provided by `ktlint-ruleset-standard` in Ktlint `0.49.x` the suggested refactoring is as follows:
 14â†’
 15â†’* Replace `LeafElement.replaceWithText(String)` with `LeafElement.rawReplaceWithText(String)`.
 16â†’* Replace `PsiElement.addAfter(PsiElement, PsiElement)` with `AstNode.addChild(AstNode, AstNode)`. Note that this method inserts the new node (first) argument *before* the second argument node and as of that is not a simple replacement of the `PsiElement.addAfter(PsiElement, PsiElement)`.
 17â†’* Replace `PsiElement.replace(PsiElement)` with a sequence of `AstNode.addChild(AstNode, AstNode)` and `AstNode.removeChild(AstNode)`.
 18â†’
 19â†’Be aware that your custom rules might use other functions which also throw exceptions when the extension point `org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler` is no longer supported.
 20â†’
 21â†’In order to help you to analyse and fix the problems with your custom rules, ktlint temporarily supports to disable the extension point `org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler` using a flag. This flag is available in the Ktlint CLI and in the `KtlintRuleEngine`. By default, the extension point is enabled like it was in previous versions of ktlint.
 22â†’
 23â†’At least you should analyse the problems by running your test suits by running ktlint and disabling the extension point. Next you can start with fixing and releasing the updated rules. All rules in this version of ktlint have already been refactored and are not dependent on the extension point anymore. In Ktlint CLI the flag is to be activated with parameter `--disable-kotlin-extension-point`. API Consumers that use the `KtlintRuleEngine` directly, have to set property `enableKotlinCompilerExtensionPoint` to `false`.
 24â†’
 25â†’At this point in time, it is not yet decided what the next steps will be. Ktlint might drop the support of the extension points entirely. Or, if the extension point `org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler` is fully supported at the time that ktlint will be based on kotlin 1.9 it might be kept. In either case, the flag will be dropped in a next version of ktlint.
 26â†’
 27â†’### Added
 28â†’
 29â†’* Add new experimental rule `binary-expression-wrapping`. This rule wraps a binary expression in case the max line length is exceeded ([#1940](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1940))
 30â†’* Add flag to disable extension point `org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler` to analyse impact on custom rules [#1981](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1981)
 31â†’* Add new experimental rule `no-empty-file` for all code styles. A kotlin (script) file may not be empty ([#1074](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1074))
 32â†’* Add new experimental rule `statement-wrapping` which ensures function, class, or other blocks statement body doesn't start or end at starting or ending braces of the block ([#1938](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1938))
 33â†’* Add new experimental rule `blank-line-before-declaration`. This rule requires a blank line before class, function or property declarations ([#1939](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1939))
 34â†’
 35â†’### Removed
 36â†’
 37â†’### Fixed
 38â†’
 39â†’* Do not flag a (potential) mutable extension property in case the getter is annotated or prefixed with a modifier `property-naming` ([#2024](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2024))
 40â†’* Do not merge an annotated expression body with the function signature even if it fits on a single line ([#2043](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2043))

 42â†’* Prevent incorrect reporting of violations in case a nullable function type reference exceeds the maximum line length `parameter-list-wrapping` ([#1324](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1324)) 
 43â†’* Prevent false negative on `else` branch when body contains only chained calls or binary expression ([#2057](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2057))
 44â†’

Although the classes were marked with `@JvmInline` it seems that it is not possible to uses those classes from Java base API Consumers like Spotless. The classes have now been replaced with data classes ([#2041](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2041))

 80â†’WARNING: This version of KtLint contains a number of breaking changes in KtLint CLI and KtLint API. If you are using KtLint with custom ruleset jars or custom reporter jars, then those need to be upgraded before you can use them with this version of ktlint. Please contact the maintainers of those jars and ask them to upgrade a.s.a.p.
 81â†’
 82â†’All rule id's in the output of Ktlint are now prefixed with a rule set. Although KtLint currently supports standard rules to be unqualified, users are encouraged to include the rule set id in all references to rules. This includes following:

See [disabled rules](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/configuration-ktlint/#disabled-rules) for more information.
 85â†’* The `source` element in the KtLint CLI `baseline.xml` file. Regenerating this file, fixes all rule references automatically.
 86â†’* The KtLint disable directives `ktlint-enable` / `ktlint-disable` and the `@Suppress('ktlint:...')` annotations.
 87â†’* The `VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule`.

See [disabled rules](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/configuration-ktlint/#disabled-rules) for more information.

125â†’        require(traversalState == TraversalState.NOT_STARTED)
126â†’        traversalState = TraversalState.CONTINUE
127â†’    }
128â†’
129â†’    /**
130â†’     * Checks whether the next node in the AST is to be traversed. By default, the entire AST is traversed.
131â†’     */
132â†’    public fun shouldContinueTraversalOfAST(): Boolean = traversalState == TraversalState.CONTINUE
133â†’
134â†’    /**
135â†’     * Stops traversal of the AST. Intended usage it to prevent parsing of the remainder of the AST once the goal of the
136â†’     * rule is achieved. For example, if the ".editorconfig" property indent_size is set to 0 or -1 then the indent rule
137â†’     * should be disabled.
138â†’     *
139â†’     * When called in [beforeFirstNode], no AST nodes will be visited. [afterLastNode] is still called.
140â†’     *
141â†’     * When called in [beforeVisitChildNodes], the child nodes of that node will not be visited. [afterVisitChildNodes]
142â†’     * is still called for the node and each of its parent nodes. Other nodes in the AST will not be visited. Finally
143â†’     * [afterLastNode] is called.
144â†’     *
145â†’     * When called in [afterVisitChildNodes] the child nodes of that node are already visited. [afterVisitChildNodes] is
146â†’     * still called for each of its parent nodes. Other nodes in the AST will not be visited. Finally [afterLastNode] is
147â†’     * called.
148â†’     *
149â†’     * Calling in [afterLastNode] has no effect as traversal of the AST has already been completed.
150â†’     */
151â†’    public fun stopTraversalOfAST() {
152â†’        traversalState = TraversalState.STOP
153â†’    }
154â†’
155â†’    private enum class TraversalState {
156â†’        /**
157â†’         * Traversal of the AST is not started. As no life cycle hooks of the [Rule] have been executed, the [Rule]
158â†’         * instance can not contain state specific for the AST.
159â†’         */
160â†’        NOT_STARTED,
161â†’
162â†’        /**
163â†’         * Traversal of the AST is started and should be continued with next node.
164â†’         */
165â†’        CONTINUE,
166â†’
167â†’        /**
168â†’         * Stops traversal of yet unvisited nodes in the AST. See [stopTraversalOfAST] for more details.
169â†’         */
170â†’        STOP,
171â†’    }

 11â†’## Binary expression wrapping
 12â†’
 13â†’Wraps binary expression at the operator reference whenever the binary expression does not fit on the line. In case the binary expression is nested, the expression is evaluated from outside to inside. If the left and right hand sides of the binary expression, after wrapping, fit on a single line then the inner binary expressions will not be wrapped. If one or both inner binary expression still do not fit on a single after wrapping of the outer binary expression, then each of those inner binary expressions will be wrapped.
 14â†’
 15â†’=== "[:material-heart:](#) Ktlint"
 16â†’
 17â†’    ```kotlin
 18â†’    fun foo() {
 19â†’        // Assume that the last allowed character is
 20â†’        // at the X character on the right                       X
 21â†’        if ((leftHandSideExpression && rightHandSideExpression) ||
 22â†’            (leftHandSideLongExpression &&
 23â†’                rightHandSideLongExpression)) {
 24â†’            // do something
 25â†’        }
 26â†’    }
 27â†’    ```
 28â†’
 29â†’=== "[:material-heart-off-outline:](#) Disallowed"
 30â†’
 31â†’    ```kotlin
 32â†’    fun foo() {
 33â†’        // Assume that the last allowed character is
 34â†’        // at the X character on the right                       X
 35â†’        if ((leftHandSideExpression && rightHandSideExpression) || (leftHandSideLongExpression && rightHandSideLongExpression)) {
 36â†’            // do something
 37â†’        }
 38â†’    }
 39â†’    ```
 40â†’
 41â†’Rule id: `binary-expression-wrapping` (`standard` rule set)
 42â†’
 43â†’## Blank line before declarations
 44â†’
 45â†’Requires a blank line before any class or function declaration. No blank line is required between the class signature and the first declaration in the class. In a similar way, a blank line is required before any list of top level or class properties. No blank line is required before local properties or between consecutive properties.

 55â†’
 56â†’        fun bar1() {
 57â†’           val foo4 = "foo4"
 58â†’           val foo5 = "foo5"
 59â†’        }
 60â†’
 61â†’        fun bar2() = "bar"
 62â†’
 63â†’        val foo6 = "foo3"
 64â†’        val foo7 = "foo4"
 65â†’
 66â†’        enum class Foo {}
 67â†’    }
 68â†’    ```

 93â†’## Discouraged comment location
 94â†’
 95â†’Detect discouraged comment locations (no autocorrect).
 96â†’
 97â†’!!! note
 98â†’    Kotlin allows comments to be placed almost everywhere. As this can lead to code which is hard to read, most of them will never be used in practice. Ideally each rule takes comments at all possible locations into account. Sometimes this is really hard and not worth the effort. By explicitly forbidding such comment locations, the development of those rules becomes a bit easier. 
 99â†’
100â†’=== "[:material-heart-off-outline:](#) Disallowed"
101â†’
102â†’    ```kotlin
103â†’    fun <T> /* some comment */ foo(t: T) = "some-result"
104â†’
105â†’    fun foo() {
106â†’        if (true)
107â†’            // some comment
108â†’            bar()
109â†’    }
110â†’    ```
111â†’
112â†’Rule id: `discouraged-comment-location` (`standard` rule set)
113â†’

115â†’

122â†’        val foo = "foo"
123â†’    }
124â†’    ```
125â†’
126â†’=== "[:material-heart-off-outline:](#) Disallowed"

130â†’
131â†’        val foo = "foo"
132â†’    }
133â†’    ```

140â†’## Disallow consecutive comments
141â†’
142â†’Consecutive comments are disallowed in following cases:
143â†’- Any mix of a consecutive kdoc, a block comment or an EOL comment unless separated by a blank line in between
144â†’- Consecutive KDocs (even when separated by a blank line)
145â†’- Consecutive block comments (even when separated by a blank line)
146â†’
147â†’Consecutive EOL comments are always allowed as they are often used instead of a block comment.
148â†’
149â†’=== "[:material-heart:](#) Ktlint"
150â†’
151â†’    ```kotlin
152â†’    // An EOL comment
153â†’    // may be followed by another EOL comment
154â†’    val foo = "foo"
155â†’
156â†’    // Different comment types (including KDoc) may be consecutive ..
157â†’
158â†’    /*
159â†’     * ... but do need to be separated by a blank line ...
160â†’     */
161â†’
162â†’    /**
163â†’      * ... but a KDoc can not be followed by an EOL or a block comment or another KDoc
164â†’      */
165â†’    fun bar() = "bar"
166â†’
167â†’    ```
168â†’
169â†’=== "[:material-heart-off-outline:](#) Disallowed"
170â†’
171â†’    ```kotlin
172â†’    /*
173â†’     * Block comments can not be consecutive ...
174â†’     */
175â†’    /*
176â†’     * ... even not when separated by a new line.
177â†’     */
178â†’    val bar = "bar" 
179â†’
180â†’    /**
181â†’      * A KDoc can not be followed by a block comment or an EOL comment or another KDOC
182â†’      */
183â†’
184â†’    // ... even not when separated by a new line.
185â†’    ```
186â†’
187â†’Rule id: `no-consecutive-comments` (`standard` rule set)
188â†’
189â†’!!! Note
190â†’    This rule is only run when `ktlint_code_style` is set to `ktlint_official` or when the rule is enabled explicitly.

192â†’## Function signature
193â†’
194â†’Rewrites the function signature to a single line when possible (e.g. when not exceeding the `max_line_length` property) or a multiline signature otherwise. In case of function with a body expression, the body expression is placed on the same line as the function signature when not exceeding the `max_line_length` property. Optionally the function signature can be forced to be written as a multiline signature in case the function has more than a specified number of parameters (`.editorconfig` property `ktlint_function_signature_wrapping_rule_always_with_minimum_parameters`)
195â†’
196â†’=== "[:material-heart:](#) Ktlint"

200â†’    // at the X character on the right           X

202â†’        a: Any,
203â†’        b: Any,
204â†’        c: Any
205â†’    ): String {
206â†’        // body
207â†’    }
208â†’
209â†’    // Assume that the last allowed character is
210â†’    // at the X character on the right           X
211â†’    fun bar(a: Any, b: Any, c: Any): String {
212â†’        // body
213â†’    }
214â†’
215â†’    // When wrapping of body is set to 'default'.
216â†’    // Assume that the last allowed character is
217â†’    // at the X character on the right           X
218â†’    fun f(a: Any, b: Any): String = "some-result"
219â†’        .uppercase()
220â†’
221â†’    // When wrapping of body is set to 'multiline'
222â†’    // or 'always'.
223â†’    // Assume that the last allowed character is
224â†’    // at the X character on the right           X

226â†’        "some-result"
227â†’            .uppercase()
228â†’    ```

265â†’## If else bracing
266â†’
267â†’If at least one branch of an if-else statement or an if-else-if statement is wrapped between curly braces then all branches should be wrapped between braces.
268â†’
269â†’=== "[:material-heart:](#) Ktlint"
270â†’
271â†’    ```kotlin
272â†’    fun foo(value: int) {
273â†’        if (value > 0) {
274â†’            doSomething()
275â†’        } else if (value < 0) {
276â†’            doSomethingElse()
277â†’        } else {
278â†’            doSomethingElse2()
279â†’        }
280â†’    }
281â†’    ```
282â†’
283â†’=== "[:material-heart-off-outline:](#) Disallowed"
284â†’
285â†’    ```kotlin
286â†’    fun foo(value: int) {
287â†’        if (value > 0)
288â†’            doSomething()
289â†’        else if (value < 0) {
290â†’            doSomethingElse()
291â†’        } else
292â†’            doSomethingElse2()
293â†’    }
294â†’    ```
295â†’
296â†’Rule id: `if-else-bracing` (`standard` rule set)
297â†’
298â†’!!! Note
299â†’    This rule is only run when `ktlint_code_style` is set to `ktlint_official` or when the rule is enabled explicitly.
300â†’
301â†’## If else wrapping
302â†’
303â†’A single line if-statement should be kept simple. It may contain no more than one else-branch. The branches may not be wrapped in a block.
304â†’
305â†’=== "[:material-heart:](#) Ktlint"
306â†’
307â†’    ```kotlin
308â†’    fun foobar() {
309â†’        if (true) foo()
310â†’        if (true) foo() else bar()
311â†’    }
312â†’    ```
313â†’
314â†’=== "[:material-heart-off-outline:](#) Disallowed"
315â†’
316â†’    ```kotlin
317â†’    fun foobar() {
318â†’        if (true) if (false) foo() else bar()
319â†’        if (true) bar() else if (false) foo() else bar()
320â†’        if (true) { foo() } else bar()
321â†’        if (true) bar() else { if (false) foo() else bar() }
322â†’    }
323â†’    ```
324â†’
325â†’Rule id: `if-else-wrapping` (`standard` rule set)
326â†’
327â†’!!! Note
328â†’    This rule is only run when `ktlint_code_style` is set to `ktlint_official` or when the rule is enabled explicitly.
329â†’
330â†’## Naming
331â†’
332â†’### Function naming
333â†’
334â†’Enforce naming of function. 
335â†’
336â†’=== "[:material-heart:](#) Ktlint"
337â†’
338â†’    ```kotlin
339â†’    fun foo() {}
340â†’    fun fooBar() {}
341â†’    ```
342â†’=== "[:material-heart:](#) Ktlint Test"
343â†’
344â†’    ```kotlin
345â†’    @Test
346â†’    fun `Some name`() {}
347â†’
348â†’    @Test
349â†’    fun do_something() {}
350â†’    ```
351â†’
352â†’=== "[:material-heart-off-outline:](#) Disallowed"
353â†’
354â†’    ```kotlin
355â†’    fun Foo() {}
356â†’    fun Foo_Bar() {}
357â†’    fun `Some name`() {}
358â†’    fun do_something() {}
359â†’    ```
360â†’
361â†’!!! note
362â†’    Functions in files which import a class from package `org.junit`, `org.testng` or `kotlin.test` are considered to be test functions. Functions in such classes are allowed to have underscores in the name. Or function names can be specified between backticks and do not need to adhere to the normal naming convention.
363â†’
364â†’This rule can also be suppressed with the IntelliJ IDEA inspection suppression `FunctionName`.
365â†’
366â†’Rule id: `function-naming` (`standard` rule set)
367â†’
368â†’### Package naming
369â†’
370â†’Enforce naming of package.
371â†’
372â†’This rule can also be suppressed with the IntelliJ IDEA inspection suppression `PackageName`.
373â†’
374â†’=== "[:material-heart:](#) Ktlint"
375â†’
376â†’    ```kotlin
377â†’    package foo
378â†’    package foo.foo
379â†’    package foo_bar
380â†’    package foo.foo_bar
381â†’    ```
382â†’=== "[:material-heart-off-outline:](#) Disallowed"
383â†’
384â†’    ```kotlin
385â†’    package Foo
386â†’    package foo.Foo
387â†’    package `foo bar`
388â†’    package foo.`foo bar`
389â†’    ```
390â†’
391â†’Rule id: `package-naming` (`standard` rule set)
392â†’
393â†’### Property naming
394â†’
395â†’Enforce naming of property.
396â†’
397â†’=== "[:material-heart:](#) Ktlint"
398â†’
399â†’    ```kotlin
400â†’    val FOO_1 = Foo()
401â†’    val FOO_BAR_1 = "FOO-BAR"
402â†’
403â†’    var foo1: Foo = Foo()
404â†’
405â†’    class Bar {
406â†’        const val FOO_2 = "foo"
407â†’        const val FOO_BAR_2 = "FOO-BAR"
408â†’
409â†’        val foo2 = "foo"
410â†’        val fooBar2 = "foo-bar"
411â†’
412â†’        // Backing property
413â†’        private val _elementList = mutableListOf<Element>()
414â†’        val elementList: List<Element>
415â†’            get() = _elementList
416â†’    }
417â†’    ```
418â†’=== "[:material-heart-off-outline:](#) Disallowed"
419â†’
420â†’    ```kotlin
421â†’    val Foo1 = Foo()
422â†’    val FooBar1 = "FOO-BAR"
423â†’
424â†’    var FOO_1: Foo = Foo()
425â†’
426â†’    class Bar {
427â†’        const val foo2 = "foo"
428â†’        const val fooBar2 = "FOO-BAR"
429â†’
430â†’        val FOO2 = "foo"
431â†’        val FOO_BAR_2 = "foo-bar"
432â†’
433â†’        // Incomplete backing property as public property 'elementList1' is missing
434â†’        private val _elementList1 = mutableListOf<Element>()
435â†’
436â†’        // Invalid backing property as '_elementList2' is not a private property
437â†’        val _elementList2 = mutableListOf<Element>()
438â†’        val elementList2: List<Element>
439â†’            get() = _elementList2
440â†’    }
441â†’    ```
442â†’
443â†’!!! note
444â†’    Top level `val` properties and `const val` properties have to be written in screaming snake notation. Local `val` and `const val` are written in lower camel case.
445â†’
446â†’This rule can also be suppressed with the IntelliJ IDEA inspection suppression `PropertyName`.
447â†’
448â†’Rule id: `property-naming` (`standard` rule set)
449â†’
450â†’## No empty file
451â†’
452â†’A kotlin (script) file should not be empty. It needs to contain at least one declaration. Files only contain a package and/or import statements are as of that disallowed.
453â†’
454â†’Rule id: `no-empty-file`
455â†’
456â†’## No single line block comments
457â†’
458â†’A single line block comment should be replaced with an EOL comment when possible.
459â†’
460â†’=== "[:material-heart:](#) Ktlint"
461â†’
462â†’    ```kotlin
463â†’    /*
464â†’     * Some comment

 31â†’/**
 32â†’ * https://kotlinlang.org/docs/reference/coding-conventions.html#method-call-formatting
 33â†’ *
 34â†’ * The rule is more aggressive in inserting newlines after arguments than mentioned in the styleguide:
 35â†’ * Each argument should be on a separate line if
 36â†’ * - at least one of the arguments is
 37â†’ * - maxLineLength exceeded (and separating arguments with \n would actually help)
 38â†’ * in addition, "(" and ")" must be on separates line if any of the arguments are (otherwise on the same)
 39â†’ */
 40â†’public class ArgumentListWrappingRule :
 41â†’    StandardRule(
 42â†’        id = "argument-list-wrapping",
 43â†’        visitorModifiers =
 44â†’            setOf(
 45â†’                // ArgumentListWrapping should only be used in case the max_line_length is still violated after running rules below:
 46â†’                VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule(
 47â†’                    ruleId = WRAPPING_RULE_ID,
 48â†’                    mode = REGARDLESS_WHETHER_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_OR_DISABLED,
 49â†’                ),
 50â†’                VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule(
 51â†’                    ruleId = BINARY_EXPRESSION_WRAPPING_RULE_ID,
 52â†’                    mode = REGARDLESS_WHETHER_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_OR_DISABLED,
 53â†’                ),
 54â†’            ),
 55â†’        usesEditorConfigProperties =
 56â†’            setOf(
 57â†’                INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
 58â†’                INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 59â†’                MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY,
 60â†’            ),
 61â†’    ) {
 62â†’    private var editorConfigIndent = IndentConfig.DEFAULT_INDENT_CONFIG
 63â†’
 64â†’    private var maxLineLength = MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY.defaultValue
 65â†’
 66â†’    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
 67â†’        editorConfigIndent =
 68â†’            IndentConfig(
 69â†’                indentStyle = editorConfig[INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY],
 70â†’                tabWidth = editorConfig[INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY],
 71â†’            )
 72â†’        maxLineLength = editorConfig[MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY]
 73â†’    }
 74â†’
 75â†’    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 76â†’        node: ASTNode,
 77â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 78â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 79â†’    ) {
 80â†’        if (editorConfigIndent.disabled) {
 81â†’            return
 82â†’        }
 83â†’
 84â†’        if (node.elementType == VALUE_ARGUMENT_LIST) {
 85â†’            if (needToWrapArgumentList(node)) {
 86â†’                node
 87â†’                    .children()
 88â†’                    .forEach { child -> wrapArgumentInList(child, emit, autoCorrect) }
 89â†’            }
 90â†’        }
 91â†’    }
 92â†’
 93â†’    private fun needToWrapArgumentList(node: ASTNode) =
 94â†’        if ( // skip when there are no arguments
 95â†’            node.firstChildNode?.treeNext?.elementType != ElementType.RPAR &&
 96â†’            // skip lambda arguments
 97â†’            node.treeParent?.elementType != ElementType.FUNCTION_LITERAL &&
 98â†’            // skip if number of arguments is big (we assume it with a magic number of 8)
 99â†’            node.children().count { it.elementType == ElementType.VALUE_ARGUMENT } <= 8
100â†’        ) {
101â†’            // each argument should be on a separate line if
102â†’            // - at least one of the arguments is
103â†’            // - maxLineLength exceeded (and separating arguments with \n would actually help)
104â†’            // in addition, "(" and ")" must be on separates line if any of the arguments are (otherwise on the same)
105â†’            node.textContainsIgnoringLambda('\n') || node.exceedsMaxLineLength()
106â†’        } else {
107â†’            false
108â†’        }
109â†’
110â†’    private fun ASTNode.exceedsMaxLineLength() = (column - 1 + textLength) > maxLineLength && !textContains('\n')
111â†’
112â†’    private fun intendedIndent(child: ASTNode): String =
113â†’        when {
114â†’            // IDEA quirk:
115â†’            // generic<
116â†’            //     T,
117â†’            //     R>(
118â†’            //     1,
119â†’            //     2
120â†’            // )
121â†’            // instead of
122â†’            // generic<
123â†’            //     T,
124â†’            //     R>(
125â†’            //         1,
126â†’            //         2
127â†’            //     )
128â†’            child.treeParent.hasTypeArgumentListInFront() -> -1
129â†’
130â†’            // IDEA quirk:
131â†’            // foo
132â†’            //     .bar = Baz(
133â†’            //     1,
134â†’            //     2
135â†’            // )
136â†’            // instead of
137â†’            // foo
138â†’            //     .bar = Baz(
139â†’            //         1,
140â†’            //         2
141â†’            //     )
142â†’            child.treeParent.isPartOfDotQualifiedAssignmentExpression() -> -1
143â†’
144â†’            else -> 0
145â†’        }.let {
146â†’            if (child.treeParent.isOnSameLineAsControlFlowKeyword()) {
147â†’                it + 1
148â†’            } else {
149â†’                it
150â†’            }
151â†’        }.let {
152â†’            if (child.elementType == ElementType.VALUE_ARGUMENT) {
153â†’                it + 1
154â†’            } else {
155â†’                it
156â†’            }
157â†’        }.let { indentLevelFix ->
158â†’            val indentLevel =
159â†’                editorConfigIndent
160â†’                    .indentLevelFrom(child.treeParent.indent(false))
161â†’                    .plus(indentLevelFix)
162â†’            "\n" + editorConfigIndent.indent.repeat(indentLevel)
163â†’        }
164â†’
165â†’    private fun wrapArgumentInList(
166â†’        child: ASTNode,
167â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
168â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
169â†’    ) {
170â†’        when (child.elementType) {
171â†’            ElementType.LPAR -> {
172â†’                val prevLeaf = child.prevLeaf()
173â†’                if (prevLeaf is PsiWhiteSpace && prevLeaf.textContains('\n')) {
174â†’                    emit(child.startOffset, errorMessage(child), true)
175â†’                    if (autoCorrect) {
176â†’                        prevLeaf.delete()
177â†’                    }
178â†’                }
179â†’            }
180â†’            ElementType.VALUE_ARGUMENT,
181â†’            ElementType.RPAR,
182â†’            -> {
183â†’                // aiming for
184â†’                // ... LPAR
185â†’                // <line indent + indentSize> VALUE_PARAMETER...
186â†’                // <line indent> RPAR
187â†’                val intendedIndent = intendedIndent(child)
188â†’                val prevLeaf = child.prevWhiteSpaceWithNewLine() ?: child.prevLeaf()
189â†’                if (prevLeaf is PsiWhiteSpace) {
190â†’                    if (prevLeaf.getText().contains("\n")) {
191â†’                        // The current child is already wrapped to a new line. Checking and fixing the
192â†’                        // correct size of the indent is the responsibility of the IndentationRule.
193â†’                        return
194â†’                    } else {
195â†’                        // The current child needs to be wrapped to a newline.
196â†’                        emit(child.startOffset, errorMessage(child), true)
197â†’                        if (autoCorrect) {
198â†’                            // The indentation is purely based on the previous leaf only. Note that in
199â†’                            // autoCorrect mode the indent rule, if enabled, runs after this rule and
200â†’                            // determines the final indentation. But if the indent rule is disabled then the
201â†’                            // indent of this rule is kept.
202â†’                            (prevLeaf as LeafPsiElement).rawReplaceWithText(intendedIndent)
203â†’                        }
204â†’                    }
205â†’                } else {
206â†’                    // Insert a new whitespace element in order to wrap the current child to a new line.
207â†’                    emit(child.startOffset, errorMessage(child), true)
208â†’                    if (autoCorrect) {
209â†’                        child.treeParent.addChild(PsiWhiteSpaceImpl(intendedIndent), child)
210â†’                    }
211â†’                }
212â†’                // Indentation of child nodes need to be fixed by the IndentationRule.
213â†’            }
214â†’        }
215â†’    }
216â†’
217â†’    private fun errorMessage(node: ASTNode) =
218â†’        when (node.elementType) {
219â†’            ElementType.LPAR -> """Unnecessary newline before "(""""
220â†’            ElementType.VALUE_ARGUMENT ->
221â†’                "Argument should be on a separate line (unless all arguments can fit a single line)"
222â†’            ElementType.RPAR -> """Missing newline before ")""""
223â†’            else -> throw UnsupportedOperationException()
224â†’        }

226â†’    private fun ASTNode.textContainsIgnoringLambda(char: Char): Boolean {
227â†’        return children().any { child ->
228â†’            val elementType = child.elementType
229â†’            elementType == ElementType.WHITE_SPACE && child.textContains(char) ||
230â†’                elementType == ElementType.COLLECTION_LITERAL_EXPRESSION && child.textContains(char) ||
231â†’                elementType == ElementType.VALUE_ARGUMENT && child.children().any { it.textContainsIgnoringLambda(char) }
232â†’        }
233â†’    }
234â†’
235â†’    private fun ASTNode.hasTypeArgumentListInFront(): Boolean =
236â†’        treeParent.children()
237â†’            .firstOrNull { it.elementType == ElementType.TYPE_ARGUMENT_LIST }
238â†’            ?.children()
239â†’            ?.any { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() } == true

241â†’    private fun ASTNode.isPartOfDotQualifiedAssignmentExpression(): Boolean =
242â†’        treeParent?.treeParent?.elementType == ElementType.BINARY_EXPRESSION &&
243â†’            treeParent?.treeParent?.children()?.find { it.elementType == ElementType.DOT_QUALIFIED_EXPRESSION } != null

246â†’        var prev = prevLeaf()
247â†’        while (prev != null && (prev.isWhiteSpace() || prev.isPartOfComment())) {
248â†’            if (prev.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()) {
249â†’                return prev
250â†’            }
251â†’            prev = prev.prevLeaf()
252â†’        }
253â†’        return null
254â†’    }

256â†’    private fun ASTNode.isOnSameLineAsControlFlowKeyword(): Boolean {
257â†’        val containerNode = psi.getStrictParentOfType<KtContainerNode>() ?: return false
258â†’        if (containerNode.node.elementType == ELSE) return false
259â†’        val controlFlowKeyword =
260â†’            when (val parent = containerNode.parent) {
261â†’                is KtIfExpression -> parent.ifKeyword.node
262â†’                is KtWhileExpression -> parent.firstChild.node
263â†’                is KtDoWhileExpression -> parent.whileKeyword?.node
264â†’                else -> null
265â†’            } ?: return false
266â†’
267â†’        var prevLeaf = prevLeaf() ?: return false
268â†’        while (prevLeaf != controlFlowKeyword) {
269â†’            if (prevLeaf.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()) return false
270â†’            prevLeaf = prevLeaf.prevLeaf() ?: return false
271â†’        }
272â†’        return true
273â†’    }

1â†’# Release testing
  2â†’
  3â†’Before releasing a new version of KtLint, the release candidate is tested on a selection of open source projects to improve the quality of the release, and the documentation is tested for dead links.
  4â†’
  5â†’## Setup infrastructure
  6â†’
  7â†’1. Create a directory in which the projects are grouped:
  8â†’   ```shell
  9â†’   mkdir sample-projects
 10â†’   cd sample-projects
 11â†’   ```
 12â†’2. In the `sample-projects` directory, create a shell command script `exec-in-each-project.sh` with content below. This script is used to run the same command in each of the sample projects:
 13â†’   ```shell
 14â†’   # Examples of usage
 15â†’   #
 16â†’   #   Outstanding changes per project
 17â†’   #     ./exec-in-each-project.sh "git status"
 18â†’   #
 19â†’   #   Rollback outstanding changes per project
 20â†’   #     ./exec-in-each-project.sh "git reset --hard"
 21â†’   #
 22â†’   #   Run ktlint standard rules
 23â†’   #     ktlint --version && ktlint "**/*.kt" -v --relative -F
 24â†’   #
 25â†’   #   Commit changes of standard rules:
 26â†’   #     ./exec-in-each-project.sh "git commit -m \"ktlint (0.43.2) -F\""
 27â†’
 28â†’   find . -maxdepth 1 -type d \( ! -name . \) -exec bash -c "cd '{}' && pwd && $@" \;
 29â†’   ```
 30â†’3. Clone the repositories of the sample projects (feel free to change this selection):
 31â†’   ```shell
 32â†’   git clone https://github.com/google/iosched.git
 33â†’   git clone https://github.com/android/uamp.git
 34â†’   git clone https://github.com/siempredelao/StaticAnalysisTest
 35â†’   git clone https://github.com/Kotlin/kotlinx.coroutines.git
 36â†’   git clone https://github.com/square/okhttp.git
 37â†’   git clone https://github.com/detekt/detekt.git
 38â†’   git clone https://github.com/ExpediaGroup/graphql-kotlin.git
 39â†’   git clone https://github.com/Kotlin/kotlinx.serialization.git
 40â†’   ```
 41â†’4. Create an alias or script to run the latest released version of ktlint (note that this script will print the version as reference to which version is used):
 42â†’   ```shell
 43â†’   alias ktlint-prev="ktlint-0.47.1 $@" # Replace with the latest release version
 44â†’   ```
 45â†’   Note that `~/git/ktlint` is the directory in which the ktlint project is checked out and that `~/git/ktlint/ktlint` refers to the `ktlint` CLI-module.
 46â†’5. Create an alias or script to run the latest development-version of ktlint (note that this script will print the version and the datetime of compilation as reference to which version is used):
 47â†’   ```shell
 48â†’   alias ktlint-dev="echo 'ktlint-dev version:';ls -aslh ~/git/ktlint/ktlint/build-cli/run/ktlint;echo ' ';~/git/ktlint/ktlint/build/run/ktlint $@"
 49â†’   ```
 50â†’   Note that `~/git/ktlint` is the directory in which the ktlint project is checked out and that `~/git/ktlint/ktlint` refers to the `ktlint` CLI-module.
 51â†’
 52â†’## Cleanup from previous testing
 53â†’
 54â†’1. Reset the repositories of the sample projects:
 55â†’   ```shell
 56â†’   ./exec-in-each-project.sh "git reset --hard origin"
 57â†’   ```
 58â†’2. Optionally, pull latest changes of the sample projects:
 59â†’   ```shell
 60â†’   ./exec-in-each-project.sh "git pull"
 61â†’   ```
 62â†’3. Remove baseline file
 63â†’   ```shell
 64â†’   rm baseline.xml
 65â†’   ```
 66â†’
 67â†’## Testing a new release
 68â†’
 69â†’Pre-requisites:
 70â†’* All changes have been merged to master.
 71â†’* Latest version of master has been pulled to the local machine.
 72â†’* The latest released version of ktlint matches with command `ktlint`:
 73â†’   ```shell
 74â†’   ktlint-prev --version
 75â†’   ```
 76â†’* The latest version of master has been build on the local machine. And it is verified that the datetime of `ktlint-dev` (more or less) matches with the datetime of this compilation.
 77â†’   ```shell
 78â†’   ktlint-dev --version
 79â†’   ```
 80â†’
 81â†’Formatting projects in which ktlint is not used may result in a huge amount of fixes. The main focus of this test is to see what the effects are when upgrading ktlint in a project already formatted with latest released ktlint version.
 82â†’
 83â†’1. Format the sample projects with the *latest released* ktlint version:
 84â†’   ```shell
 85â†’   ktlint-prev -F --relative # Do not call this command via the "./exec-in-each-project.sh" script.
 86â†’   ```
 87â†’   Note: Ignore all output as this is the old version!
 88â†’2. Commit changes:
 89â†’   ```shell
 90â†’   ./exec-in-each-project.sh "git add --all && git commit -m \"Format with ktlint (xx.yy.zz) -F\""
 91â†’   ```
 92â†’   Repeat step 1 and 2 until no files are changed anymore.
 93â†’3. Create a new baseline file with the *latest released* ktlint version to ignore all lint violations that could not be autocorrected using the latest ktlint version:
 94â†’   ```shell
 95â†’   rm baseline.xml
 96â†’   ktlint-prev --baseline=baseline.xml --relative # Do not call this command via the "./exec-in-each-project.sh" script as we want one combined baseline.xml file for all projects.
 97â†’   ```
 98â†’4. Check that besides the `baseline.xml` no files are changed (in step 1 and 2 all violations which could be autocorrected have already been committed). Remaining violations which could not be autocorrected are saved in the `baseline.xml` which is stored outside the project directories.
 99â†’   ```shell
100â†’   ./exec-in-each-project.sh "git status"
101â†’   ```
102â†’   The `baseline.xml` file should only contain errors which can not be autocorrected.
103â†’5. Define `.editorconfig` in the integration test directory with content below. Also follow up the instructions mentioned:
104â†’   ```editorconfig
105â†’   root = true
106â†’   [*.{kt,kts}]
107â†’   # The open source projects that are used for release testing of ktlint contain a few '.editorconfig' files which need to
108â†’   # be changed:
109â†’   #   1) Disable the "root = true" property so that each project ultimately falls back on this file. In this way offending
110â†’   #      rules can be easily enabled/disabled for all test projects
111â†’   #   2) Add specific rules to project below
112â†’   #        graphql
113â†’   #          ktlint_standard_import-ordering = disabled
114â†’   #          ktlint_standard_package-name = disabled
115â†’   ktlint_experimental = enabled
116â†’   ktlint_standard = enabled
117â†’   ktlint_standard_filename = disabled
118â†’   ktlint_standard_no-wildcard-imports = disabled
119â†’   ktlint_standard_function-naming = disabled
120â†’   ktlint_standard_property-naming = disabled
121â†’   ```
122â†’6. Commit changes:
123â†’   ```shell
124â†’   ./exec-in-each-project.sh "git add --all && git commit -m \"Update .editorconfig to fallback to integration test settings\""
125â†’   ```
126â†’7. Lint with *latest development* version:
127â†’   ```shell
128â†’   ktlint-dev --baseline=baseline.xml --relative # Do not call this command via the "./exec-in-each-project.sh" script as we want to use the one combined baseline.xml file for all projects.
129â†’   # or when lots are violations are reported
130â†’   ktlint-dev --baseline=baseline.xml --relative --reporter=plain-summary # Do not call this command via the "./exec-in-each-project.sh" script as we want to use the one combined baseline.xml file for all projects.
131â†’   ```
132â†’   Inspect the output roughly (detailed inspection is done when formatting):
133â†’   * Is the amount of logging messages comparable to before? If not, are the changes intended?
134â†’   * Are violations related to rules that have actually been added or changed?
135â†’8. Format with *latest development* version:
136â†’   ```shell
137â†’   ktlint-dev -F --baseline=baseline.xml --relative # Do not call this command via the "./exec-in-each-project.sh" script as we want to use the one combined baseline.xml file for all projects.
138â†’   ```
139â†’   Inspect the output carefully:
140â†’    * If you see an error like below, then this version obviously may *not* be released. It is best to fix this error before continuing with testing and validating!
141â†’      ```plain
142â†’      Internal Error (...) in file '...' at position '0:0. Please create a ticket at https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues ...
143â†’      ```
144â†’   * Ideally, no violations are shown. This means that all violations have been autocorrected.
145â†’   * Violations which could not be autocorrected should be validated for correctness but do not block the release as most likely this is intended behavior.
146â†’   * If a violation is shown which is not marked as being "can not be autocorrected" this means that during autocorrect of another violation a new violations has been introduced. This should be fixed before releasing especially when the next format introduces the original violation again which of course would result in an endless loop.
147â†’9. Inspect all fixed violations, Of course inspection similar violations tens of times does not make sense. At least check different types of violations a couple of times. Commit changes which do not need to be inspected again:
148â†’   ```shell
149â†’   ./exec-in-each-project.sh "git add --all && git commit -m \"Fixed with latest development version\""
150â†’   ```
151â†’10. Rerun lint with *latest development* version:
152â†’    ```shell
153â†’    ktlint-dev --baseline=baseline.xml --relative # Do not call this command via the "./exec-in-each-project.sh" script as we want to use the one combined baseline.xml file for all projects.
154â†’    ```
155â†’    No violations, except error that can not be autocorrected, should be expected.

157â†’## Checking documentation
158â†’[The documentation for KtLint](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/) should be checked for dead links.
159â†’Follow the instructions for building the documentation in `/docs/readme.md`, and use a tool such as [Broken Link Checker Tool](https://www.deadlinkchecker.com/website-dead-link-checker.asp) to find broken links.
160â†’
